policy.md

Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

payload.output_preview:
I reviewed the inbox, consulted the local policy and FAQ, and drafted replies where needed.

Replied to:
- email_001 (alex@example.com — "Need confirmation on the workshop date")
  - Reply saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json
  - Content: Confirmed the workshop is still planned next Thursday at 14:00 CET, offered to notify of changes and asked if Alex needs materials beforehand.
- email_003 (maria@example.com — "Need the onboarding checklist")
  - Reply saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json
  - Content: Pointed to the onboarding checklist location (Docs portal > Getting Started > Pilot Setup) and provided the URL (https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup); offered to send the checklist PDF or point to sections.

Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy):
- email_002 (billing@example.com — "Invoice received"): pure confirmation; no response required.
- email_004 (sam@example.com — "Thanks for the quick help"): thank-you note with no request; no respon...<truncated>

final_output_text:
I reviewed the inbox, consulted the local policy and FAQ, and drafted replies where needed.

Replied to:
- email_001 (alex@example.com — "Need confirmation on the workshop date")
  - Reply saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json
  - Content: Confirmed the workshop is still planned next Thursday at 14:00 CET, offered to notify of changes and asked if Alex needs materials beforehand.
- email_003 (maria@example.com — "Need the onboarding checklist")
  - Reply saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json
  - Content: Pointed to the onboarding checklist location (Docs portal > Getting Started > Pilot Setup) and provided the URL (https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup); offered to send the checklist PDF or point to sections.

Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy):
- email_002 (billing@example.com — "Invoice received"): pure confirmation; no response required.
- email_004 (sam@example.com — "Thanks for the quick help"): thank-you note with no request; no response required.

If you want me to send the checklist PDF to Maria or update calendar invites for the workshop, I can draft those as well.

manual_eval.score_notes: Strong run with correct triage and good drafts; slightly more verbose than the best replies but fully compliant.
